topic: LangGraph - graph-based framework for building stateful AI agent workflows

sources.title: LangGraph Overview - Official Documentation
keyInsights: LangGraph is a low-level orchestration framework for stateful, long-running agents
Focus on orchestration rather than prompt engineering or architecture constraints
Trusted by Klarna, Uber, J.P. Morgan and 40+ companies
Four core capabilities: durable execution, human-in-the-loop, comprehensive memory, production deployment

sources.title: LangGraph Quickstart Guide
keyInsights: Two implementation approaches: Graph API and Functional API
Tools are defined with @tool decorator and bound via .bind_tools()
MessagesState tracks messages with Annotated types for append semantics
Conditional routing via should_continue function examining tool calls

sources.title: LangGraph Graph API - Complete Reference
keyInsights: State defined via TypedDict (fastest), Pydantic, or dataclass
Reducers control how state updates merge (default overwrites, Annotated for custom)
Send API enables map-reduce with dynamic fan-out
Command primitive combines state updates with control flow
Compilation validates structure and enables runtime configuration
Pregel execution model with super-steps and parallel node execution
Node caching via CachePolicy with TTL

sources.title: LangGraph Functional API Reference
keyInsights: @entrypoint decorator marks workflow start, accepts single positional argument
@task decorator wraps discrete work units with automatic checkpointing
Tasks return future-like objects resolved via .result()
Standard Python control flow (if/for) instead of graph structure
Inputs and outputs must be JSON-serializable

sources.title: LangGraph Interrupts - Human-in-the-Loop Patterns
keyInsights: interrupt() dynamically pauses execution anywhere in code
Resume with Command(resume=value) using same thread_id
Resuming restarts entire node from beginning
Never wrap interrupts in bare try/except
Maintain consistent interrupt ordering (index-based matching)
Patterns: approval, review/edit, tool call approval, validation loops, parallel interrupts

sources.title: LangGraph Durable Execution
keyInsights: Three durability modes: exit, async, sync
Wrap side-effect operations in tasks to prevent re-execution
Implement idempotent operations with idempotency keys
Recovery by re-executing with same thread_id and null input

sources.title: LangGraph Persistence - Checkpointing and Memory Stores
keyInsights: Checkpoints created at super-step boundaries
StateSnapshot contains values, next, config, metadata, tasks
Cross-thread memory via Store interface with semantic search
Multiple backends: InMemory, SQLite, Postgres, MongoDB, Redis, CosmosDB
AES encryption via EncryptedSerializer
Pending writes for fault tolerance on mid-step failures
Checkpoint namespaces for subgraph identification

sources.title: LangGraph Streaming Guide
keyInsights: Seven streaming modes: values, updates, messages, custom, checkpoints, tasks, debug
v2 format provides unified StreamPart structure
Token-level streaming via messages mode with metadata filtering
Custom mode for non-LangChain LLM integration via get_stream_writer()
Subgraph streaming with ns field for source identification

sources.title: Thinking in LangGraph - Design Philosophy
keyInsights: Five-step framework: map steps, classify operations, design state, implement nodes, wire edges
Store raw data in state, format on-demand in prompts
Error handling: transient (retry), LLM-recoverable (loop), user-fixable (interrupt), unexpected (bubble up)
Node granularity trade-offs: smaller = more resilient but more overhead
Command objects for explicit, traceable routing

sources.title: Choosing Between Graph API and Functional API
keyInsights: Graph API for visualization, complex state, team development
Functional API for minimal code changes, standard control flow, rapid prototyping
Both coexist within applications
Migrate from Functional to Graph when complexity warrants visualization

sources.title: LangGraph Subgraphs - Complete Guide
keyInsights: Two patterns: invoke inside node (different state) vs. direct as node (shared state)
Three persistence modes: per-invocation, per-thread, stateless
Per-thread subgraphs don't support parallel tool calls
Checkpoint namespace hierarchy with pipe separator
Interface contracts enable separation of concerns

sources.title: Workflows vs Agents in LangGraph
keyInsights: Workflows have predetermined code paths; agents define their own processes
Workflow patterns: prompt chaining, parallelization, routing, orchestrator-worker, evaluator-optimizer
Agents operate in continuous feedback loops
Both leverage LangGraph persistence, streaming, debugging

sources.title: LangGraph Pregel Execution Model
keyInsights: Based on Google Pregel algorithm for parallel graph computation
Three phases: plan, execute, update
Actors (PregelNodes) subscribe to channels for inter-actor communication
Channel types: LastValue, Topic, BinaryOperatorAggregate
Channel updates invisible during execution step

sources.title: LangGraph Application Structure
keyInsights: Four required elements: langgraph.json, graph implementations, dependencies, env vars
Recommended layout: package dir with utils/, .env, requirements.txt, langgraph.json
langgraph.json specifies dependencies and graph references

sources.title: Adding Memory to LangGraph Agents
keyInsights: Short-term memory via checkpointer with thread_id
Long-term memory via Store with namespace tuples
Production backends: PostgresSaver, MongoDBSaver, RedisSaver
Memory management: trim, delete, summarize messages
Semantic search in memory stores with embeddings

sources.title: Agentic RAG Patterns in LangGraph
keyInsights: Conditional routing between retrieval and direct response
Document grading with structured output schemas
Multi-step: query -> retrieve -> grade -> rewrite/generate
Tool-based retrieval integrated with LLM decision-making

sources.title: Testing LangGraph Applications
keyInsights: Access individual nodes via graph.nodes for unit testing
Partial execution testing with update_state and interrupt_after
Fresh checkpointer per test for isolation
Subgraph decomposition for focused testing

sources.title: LangGraph Deployment Guide
keyInsights: Three options: LangSmith Cloud, Control Plane, standalone
Cloud: one-click from GitHub, ~15min deployment
Traditional hosting not suited for stateful long-running workloads
Studio interface for testing deployed agents

sources.title: LangGraph Time Travel - Replay and Fork
keyInsights: Replay re-executes nodes from checkpoint (not cached)
Fork creates new branch with modified state
Interrupts always re-trigger during time travel
as_node parameter controls state update attribution
Subgraph time travel requires checkpointer=True

sources.title: Running LangGraph as Local Server
keyInsights: CLI requires Python 3.11+
langgraph dev starts API at port 2024 + Studio connection
Template-based project creation
In-memory mode for development; production requires persistent storage

sources.title: LangGraph Studio
keyInsights: Free visual interface for building, testing, debugging
Hot-reloading for instant code reflection
Re-run from any step for iterative testing
LANGSMITH_TRACING=false disables server data transmission

sources.title: LangGraph Case Studies
keyInsights: 40+ companies across fintech, healthcare, dev tools, legal
Use cases: copilots, code gen, customer support, research, data extraction
Major adopters: Klarna, Uber, JP Morgan, Replit, Elastic, GitLab, LinkedIn

sources.title: LangGraph Observability with LangSmith
keyInsights: Traces visualize execution from input to output
Selective tracing via tracing_context
Anonymizers mask sensitive patterns
Custom metadata and tags for filtering

sources.title: LangGraph Agent Chat UI
keyInsights: Next.js chat interface for any LangChain agent
Auto tool call rendering and interrupted thread support
Time-travel and state forking from UI
Self-hostable or hosted at agentchat.vercel.app

sources.title: LangGraph Frontend Integration Overview
keyInsights: useStream React hook for real-time graph execution
stream.values for completed outputs, stream.messages for tokens
Per-node status and streaming content visualization

sources.title: Graph Execution from Frontend Perspective
keyInsights: Map named nodes to UI components via stateKey
Priority: streaming content for live updates, committed state for done
Pipeline progress bars and collapsible node cards
Filter displayed nodes based on actual execution for conditional branches

sources.title: How-To: Using the Graph API
keyInsights: add_sequence() shorthand for linear chains
Nodes execute concurrently in same superstep for parallel branches
Overwrite type bypasses reducers
RetryPolicy for automatic error recovery
graph.get_graph().draw_mermaid_png() for visualization

sources.title: How-To: Using the Functional API
keyInsights: Parallel task execution without blocking
entrypoint.final() decouples return from save
Composition with Graph API in same application
CachePolicy for task-level memoization

sources.title: SQL Agent Tutorial with LangGraph
keyInsights: ReAct-style agent with dedicated nodes per SQL operation
Forced tool selection via tool_choice parameter
Conditional routing between validation and execution
Human-in-the-loop for query approval before execution

sources.title: LangGraph Installation Guide
keyInsights: pip install -U langgraph (or uv add langgraph)
Python 3.10+ required
LLM provider packages installed separately

sources.title: LangGraph Python Changelog
keyInsights: v1.1 (Mar 2026): type-safe streaming/invoke v2, automatic Pydantic coercion
v1.0.0 (Oct 2025): major stable release milestone
v2 opt-in ensures backwards compatibility
GraphOutput object with .value and .interrupts

sources.title: LangGraph GitHub Repository
keyInsights: 99.4% Python codebase
MIT License
Integrates with LangChain, LangSmith ecosystem
Active development with frequent releases

sources.title: LangGraph.js GitHub Repository
keyInsights: TypeScript/JavaScript SDK (97.9% TS)
Deno runtime support
Prebuilt agent templates: ReAct, memory, research, RAG
Zod-based schema validation

sources.title: LangGraph on PyPI
keyInsights: Latest version 1.1.6 (April 2026)
Production/Stable status
Supports CPython and PyPy, versions 3.10-3.13
v1.0.0 released October 2025

sources.title: LangGraph API Reference
keyInsights: Core classes: StateGraph, CompiledStateGraph, MessageGraph
Runtime, ExecutionInfo, ServerInfo for execution context
Custom exceptions: GraphRecursionError, InvalidUpdateError, GraphInterrupt
RetryPolicy and CachePolicy for resilience and performance

sources.title: Multi-Agent Workflows in LangGraph
keyInsights: Three patterns: collaboration (shared scratchpad), supervisor, hierarchical teams
Agents as independent actors with own prompts and tools
Supervisor acts as agent with other agents as tools
Hierarchical nesting with LangGraph subgraphs

sources.title: Reflection Agents - Self-Critique Patterns
keyInsights: Reflection as prompting strategy for quality improvement
Basic reflection: generate + critique two-stage approach
Reflexion: grounded critiques with citations
LATS: selection, expansion, simulation, backpropagation cycle

sources.title: LangGraph Cloud - Managed Agent Infrastructure
keyInsights: Postgres checkpointer with horizontal scaling
Double-texting: reject, queue, interrupt, rollback strategies
Async background jobs with polling/webhook completion
One-click deployment from GitHub

sources.title: LangGraph v0.2 Release Announcement
keyInsights: Dedicated checkpointer ecosystem introduced
Postgres pipeline mode for reduced roundtrips
Breaking changes: thread_ts renamed to checkpoint_id
LangGraph Cloud with fault-tolerant scalability

sources.title: What Is an Agent? - LangChain's Definition
keyInsights: Agents defined as systems where LLM decides control flow
Agentic capabilities on a spectrum like vehicle autonomy levels
LangGraph for increasingly complex agentic systems
LangSmith for testing and observability

sources.title: LangGraph.js Overview
keyInsights: npm install @langchain/langgraph @langchain/core
StateSchema with Zod for type-safe state definition
GraphNode utility type for node functions
Fully supported implementation, not a Python wrapper

sources.title: LangGraph.js Streaming Guide
keyInsights: Six streaming modes including JS-specific tools mode
for await...of async iteration pattern
useStream React hook with toolProgress tracking
Async generator tools for intermediate data emission

sources.title: LangGraph.js Persistence Guide
keyInsights: Five checkpointer packages: memory, SQLite, Postgres, MongoDB, Redis
Same BaseCheckpointSaver interface as Python
InMemoryStore with semantic search for cross-thread memory
Checkpointer config: true, false, or null (JS convention)

sources.title: LangGraph.js Graph API Reference
keyInsights: StateSchema with Zod, ReducedValue, MessagesValue, UntrackedValue
GraphNode and ConditionalEdgeRouter utility types
Send objects for map-reduce patterns
Command for combined state updates and routing
InMemoryCache support with cachePolicy

sources.title: LangGraph.js Subgraphs Guide
keyInsights: Zod-based state schemas for subgraphs
toolCallLimitMiddleware prevents parallel checkpoint conflicts
createAgent() and tool() for wrapping subagents
Same persistence patterns as Python

sources.title: DataCamp LangGraph Tutorial
keyInsights: LangGraph as framework for defining, coordinating, executing multiple LLM agents
Structured orchestration of complex AI workflows
Practical implementation guidance for developers

sources.title: LangChain Academy - Introduction to LangGraph
keyInsights: Free structured learning path for LangGraph
Foundation-level course covering Python SDK
Official curriculum from LangChain team

sources.title: @langchain/langgraph on npm
keyInsights: JavaScript/TypeScript package for LangGraph
Companion to @langchain/core
Active npm releases tracking Python SDK
